title: Brent crude trades above $126 per barrel as Iran tensions deepen
contents:
# Brent crude trades above $126 per barrel as Iran tensions deepen

## Overview
Brent crude traded above $126 per barrel during the evening of April 30, 2026 as the US-Iran war entered its 62nd day and Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei reasserted Iranian control over the Strait of Hormuz. The Independent live blog cited the price level alongside the running $25 billion war-cost estimate within the 21:00-22:11 UTC window.

## Market Snapshot
- Brent benchmark trades above $126 per barrel during evening session
- Cumulative running cost of the US-Iran war estimated at $25 billion
- Strait of Hormuz transit volumes remain materially below baseline
- Asian session participants hedge upside exposure on Iranian retaliation risk

## Sanctions and Compliance Context
UANI, a non-partisan advocacy group focused on Iran sanctions enforcement, noted earlier in the campaign that elevated oil prices increase the financial incentive for sanctioned-fleet activity through ship-to-ship transfers and AIS manipulation. With Brent at $126, the implied premium on dark-fleet Iranian crude grows materially, reinforcing OFAC's need for accelerated designation tempo against facilitator networks.

## Tags
oil, brent-crude, iran, sanctions, dark-fleet, strait-of-hormuz, uani
summary: Brent crude traded above $126 per barrel on the evening of April 30, 2026 amid Iranian threats over the Strait of Hormuz, expanding the financial incentive for sanctioned-fleet evasion as the running US-Iran war cost reached $25 billion.
